steps.title: Spot what creates lasting historical memory
steps.content: The passage contrasts Jessica Watson's achievements with what actually endures: “there is little that can ______ being the first to do something.” The example of Jeanne Bare reinforces this—history remembers her as the first woman to circumnavigate. The blank must express that being first is nearly unmatched in historical value.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: “Prevail over” means to surpass or be superior to. The completed sentence—”there is little that can prevail over being the first to do something”—conveys that almost nothing outweighs being first, perfectly fitting the passage's meaning. The other choices either fail to express this superiority or introduce contrary meanings. That matches choice B — B is correct.
